content: ICM curiosity-driven exploration in 2025 autonomous agents can be adapted to actively chunk dispersed low-level sensory or textual observations into consolidated high-level knowledge chunks before they enter an agent's working memory, offsetting the 3–5 chunk capacity limit that restricts reasoning. This reframes working memory constraint not as a fixed structural flaw to be scaled away, but as a drive that shapes how curiosity prioritizes what to encode for complex problem-solving.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
trigger_topic: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
